findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - file type validation relies solely on client-supplied Content-Type HTTP header (spoofable). Extension derived from attacker-controlled filename at line 108. ImageIO decode failure silently swallowed at lines 134-137, allowing non-image files to be stored.

findings.name: Improper Initialization
findings.description: The product does not initialize or incorrectly initializes a resource, which might leave the resource in an unexpected state when it is accessed or used.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:115,134-142 - byte[] photoData = null initialized to null; broad catch (Exception ex) at line 134 silently swallows RuntimeException from file.getBytes() at line 119, allowing execution to continue with photoData still null and persisted to database without null-check.

findings.name: Incorrect Calculation
findings.description: The product performs a calculation that generates incorrect or unintended results that are later used in security-critical decisions or resource management.
findings.evidence: PhotoRepository.java:34-42 - findPhotosUploadedBefore uses Oracle ROWNUM inside same subquery level as ORDER BY UPLOADED_AT DESC. ROWNUM is assigned before ORDER BY is applied, producing non-deterministic pagination results instead of the intended 10 most-recent older photos.

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties:11-12 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum / spring.datasource.password=photoalbum | src/main/resources/application-docker.properties:3-4 - same credentials | docker-compose.yml - ORACLE_PASSWORD=photoalbum, APP_USER_PASSWORD=photoalbum, SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=photoalbum
